title: First Spark
description: Learn the basics of wireless telegraphy aboard the SS Pemberton.

content.plain_text: MVS DE GLD QTH IO53 QSL K
content.decoded_meaning: MV Saint Brendan reporting position at grid IO53 (Anglesey area)

content.plain_text: SI DE GLD QTH IO51WB QSL K
content.decoded_meaning: Steamship Ireland reporting position near Cork

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: Welcome aboard Pemberton, lad. I'm your Chief — here to show you the ropes.
This is your radio room. The brass key bottom-right is your voice to the world. The porthole shows the sea. The chart top-left tracks our position.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: When a signal comes in, you'll hear Morse code. Four letter buttons will appear — pick the one you think matches what you heard.
You can also type directly on your KEYBOARD. The message repeats — don't rush. Just focus on one letter at a time. Hit REF in the top bar for the Morse alphabet.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: After decoding, you'll sometimes need to REPLY. The correct response appears on screen — you key it in Morse yourself.
SPACEBAR or tap the telegraph key: short = DOT, long hold = DASH. Press ENTER when done. The game scores your timing quality.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: One last thing before the signal comes — hit MENU to open Settings. You can slow the Morse speed (WPM) there.
Farnsworth spacing adds extra pauses between letters while keeping each letter's pattern at full speed. Useful when starting out. Now — listen up. Liverpool is calling.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: Not every signal is for us. You'll hear other ships and coast stations exchanging traffic — listen in.
Every message you log builds your picture of what's happening at sea. A good Sparks misses nothing.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: See the chart top-left? The green dot is us, moving toward Cork. Click it for a detailed view.
Time runs fast aboard — hours pass in minutes. Use the 4X button to skip quiet stretches. When a signal arrives, time drops to real speed automatically.

dialog.speaker: Chief Radio Officer
lines: That's the basics, Sparks. Keep your ears sharp and your key steady.
Tomorrow we sail for New York — seven days across the Atlantic. You'll be ready. Good luck — 73!
